requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural
Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
requirements.object: immediate effect

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to all Regional Rural Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank') as defined under
Clause (ja) of Section 5 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: Regional Rural Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank') as defined under Clause (ja)

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of the provisions of Section 29 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949
(BR Act, 1949), a bank shall in respect of all business transacted by it prepare a
balance sheet and profit and loss account as on the last working day of the year
or the period, as the case may be, in the Forms set out in the Third Schedule of
the BR Act, 1949.
requirements.object: business transacted by it prepare a balance sheet and profit and loss account as on the last working day of the year or the period,

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: Mere mention of an activity, transaction or item in instructions for
compilation does not imply that it is permitted, and the bank shall refer to the
extant statutory and regulatory requirements while determining the permissibility
or otherwise of an activity or transaction.
requirements.object: statutory and regulatory requirements while determining the permissibility or otherwise of an activity or transaction

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall follow the general instructions for the compilation of balance sheet
and profit and loss account as specified in subparagraph (1) below.
requirements.object: for the compilation of balance sheet and profit and loss account as specified in subparagraph (1) below

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank
shall ensure strict compliance with the Accounting Standards notified under the
Companies (Accounting Standards) Rules, 2021, as amended from time to
time, subject to Directions / Guidelines issued by the RBI.
requirements.object: the Accounting Standards notified under the Companies (Accounting Standards) Rules, 2021, as amended from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
b) A bank shall adopt its own methods, on a reasonable and consistent basis, for allocation of
expenditure among the segments.
requirements.object: on a reasonable and consistent basis, for allocation of expenditure among the segments

requirements.full_sentence: c) 'Treasury' shall include the entire investment portfolio.
requirements.object: portfolio

requirements.full_sentence:
d) Retail Banking shall include exposures which fulfil the four criteria of orientation, product, granularity,
and low value of individual exposures for retail exposures laid down in the Reserve Bank of India
Notes:
i) Related parties for a bank are its parent, subsidiary(ies), associates / joint ventures, Key
Management Personnel (KMP) and relatives of KMP.
requirements.object: the four criteria of orientation, product, granularity, and low value of individual exposures for retail exposures laid

requirements.full_sentence:
Relatives of KMP would be on the lines indicated in Section 45 S of the RBI Act, 1934
ii) The name and nature of related party relationship shall be disclosed, irrespective of whether there
have been transactions, where control exists within the meaning of the Standard.
requirements.object: whether there have been transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
The disclosures may be limited to aggregate
for each of the above related party categories and would pertain to the year-end position as also the
maximum position during the year.
requirements.object: for each of the above related party categories and would pertain to the year-end position as also the maximum position during

conditions:
in case a statute or regulator governing
an enterprise prohibits the enterprise from disclosing certain information
requirements.full_sentence:
Further, in case a statute or regulator governing
an enterprise prohibits the enterprise from disclosing certain information, which is required to be
disclosed, non-disclosure of such information would not be deemed as non-compliance with the
Accounting Standards.
requirements.object: of such information would not be deemed as non-compliance with the Accounting Standards

requirements.full_sentence:
Since materiality is not objectively defined, it has
been decided that all banks should ensure compliance with the provisions of
the Accounting Standard in respect of any item of prior period income or prior
period expenditure which exceeds one percent of the total income / total
expenditure of the bank if the income / expenditure is reckoned on a gross
basis or one percent of the net profit before taxes or net losses as the case
may be if the income is reckoned net of costs.
requirements.object: is reckoned net of costs

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Since the format of the profit and loss accounts of a bank prescribed in Form B
under Third Schedule to the BR Act, 1949, does not specifically provide for
disclosure of the impact of prior period items on the current year's profit and
loss, such disclosures, wherever warranted, may be made in the 'Notes to
Accounts' to the balance sheet of a bank.
requirements.object: 'Notes to Accounts' to the balance sheet of a bank

conditions:
in case of non-performing advances
and non-performing investments
requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Non-recognition of income by the bank in case of non-performing advances
and non-performing investments, in compliance with the regulatory
prescriptions of the RBI, shall not attract a qualification by the statutory
auditors as this would be in conformity with provisions of the standard, as it
recognises postponement of recognition of revenue where collectability of the
revenue is significantly uncertain.
requirements.object: the statutory auditors as this would be in conformity with provisions of the standard, as it recognises postponement of rec

requirements.full_sentence:
The issues that arise in this context have been identified and banks
shall be guided by the following while complying with the provisions of the
Standard:
requirements.object: following while complying with the provisions of the Standard:

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) As per paragraphs 9 and 21 of the Standard, a foreign currency transaction
shall be recorded on initial recognition in the reporting currency, by applying to
the foreign currency amount the exchange rate between the reporting
currency and the foreign currency at the date of the transaction.
requirements.object: recognition in the reporting currency, by applying to the foreign currency amount the exchange rate between the reporting curre

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may
face difficulty in applying the exchange rate prevailing at the date of the
transaction in respect of the items which are not being recorded in Indian
Rupees or are currently being recorded using a notional exchange rate.
requirements.object: the exchange rate prevailing at the date of the transaction in respect of the items which are not being recorded in Indian

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) A bank, which is in a position to apply the exchange rate prevailing on the
date of the transaction for recording the foreign currency transactions as
required under AS 11 shall comply with the requirements.

A bank, which has
an extensive branch network, have a high volume of foreign currency
transactions and is not fully equipped on the technology front shall be guided
by the following:
requirements.object: following:

requirements.full_sentence:
In view of the
practical difficulties which a bank may have in applying the exchange rates
at the dates of the transactions and since the Standard allows the use of a
rate that approximates the actual rate at the date of the transaction, the
bank may use average rates as detailed below:
requirements.object: exchange rates at the dates of the transactions and since the Standard allows the use of a rate that approximates the actual rate

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) In respect of those foreign currency transactions, which are currently not
being recorded in Indian Rupees at the date of the transaction or are
being recorded using a notional exchange rate shall now be recorded at
the date of the transaction by using the weekly average closing rate of the
preceding week, published by FEDAI, if the same approximates the actual
rate at the date of the transaction.
requirements.object: the date of the transaction by using the weekly average closing rate of the preceding week, published by FEDAI

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) If the weekly average closing rate of the preceding week does not
approximate the actual rate at the date of the transaction, the closing rate
at the date of the transaction shall be used.

(b) In order to ensure uniformity among banks, closing rate to be applied for the
purposes of AS 11 (revised 2003) for the relevant accounting period shall be
<image>
the last closing spot rate of exchange announced by FEDAI for that
accounting period.
requirements.object: closing spot rate of exchange announced by FEDAI for that accounting period

requirements.full_sentence:
The items listed in these Directions shall be disclosed in the 'Notes to Accounts'
to the financial statements.
requirements.object: 'Notes to Accounts' to the financial statements

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall make additional disclosures where
material.
requirements.object: material

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the schedules to the balance sheet, a summary of 'significant
accounting policies' and 'Notes to Accounts' shall be disclosed as separate

A bank shall, at the minimum, furnish the following information in the 'Notes to
Accounts'.
requirements.object: furnish the following information in the 'Notes to Accounts'

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall note that mere mention of an activity, transaction or
item in the disclosure template does not imply that it is permitted, and the bank
shall refer to the extant statutory and regulatory requirements while determining
the permissibility or otherwise of an activity or transaction.
requirements.object: of an activity, transaction or item in the disclosure template does not imply that it is permitted, and the bank shall refer to

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall
disclose comparative information in respect of the previous period for all
amounts reported in the current period's financial statements.
requirements.object: respect of the previous period for all amounts reported in the current period's financial statements

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the bank
shall include comparative information for narrative and descriptive information if
it is relevant to understanding the current period's financial statements.
requirements.object: narrative and descriptive information if it is relevant to understanding the current period's financial statements

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(1) Regulatory capital
Note: A bank shall be guided by the Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025, as amended from
time to time, for associated guidelines on asset liability.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025, as amended from time to time, for associa

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Draw down from Reserves: Suitable disclosures mentioning the amount and
the rationale for withdrawal shall be made regarding any draw down from
reserves.
requirements.object: draw down from reserves

requirements.full_sentence:
For a bank, the Total under column 3 shall match with the sum of total of Investments included
under the following categories in Schedule 8 to the balance sheet:
a) Investment in India in
i) Shares ii) Debentures and Bonds iii) Subsidiaries and / or Joint Ventures iv) Others
b) Investment outside India in (where applicable)
i) Government securities (including local authorities)
ii) Subsidiaries and / or joint ventures abroad
iii) Other investments
requirements.object: of total of Investments included under the following categories in Schedule 8 to the balance sheet: a) Investment in India in i) Sh

requirements.full_sentence: Amounts reported under columns 4, 5, 6 and 7 above may not be mutually exclusive.

Note:
Note: The disclosure shall be as specified in the Reserve Bank of India (Government Securities
Lending) Directions, 2023, as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank of India (Government Securities Lending) Directions, 2023, as amended from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Asset quality
Note -
1) While making disclosures in audited annual financial statements, a bank should invariably provide the figures for both the current and previous year to facilitate comparison.
requirements.object: for both the current and previous year to facilitate comparison

requirements.full_sentence: Amount of Technical write-off should be certified by statutory auditors.
requirements.object: auditors

requirements.full_sentence: 3) To the extent that General Provisions and Loss Reserves have not been reckoned for Tier 2 capital, they may be netted off from Gross NPAs to arrive at Net NPAs.
requirements.object: Gross NPAs to arrive at Net NPAs

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: While making disclosures in audited annual financial statements, a bank should invariably
provide the figures for both the current and previous year to facilitate comparison.
requirements.object: for both the current and previous year to facilitate comparison

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: As regards NFB credit limits, the bank shall be guided by the Reserve Bank of India (Regional
Rural Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025, as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025, as amended from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall
come into force from April 1, 2026, or from any earlier date as decided by a bank as per its internal
policy ("effective date").
requirements.object: April 1, 2026, or from any earlier date as decided by a bank as per its internal policy ("effective date")

requirements.full_sentence:
Extension of any new NFB facility and renewal of an existing NFB facility after
the effective date, shall be governed in terms of these Directions.
requirements.object: of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
All existing NFB facilities extended/
renewed till the effective date shall be governed by the existing instructions as applicable to the
respective bank.
requirements.object: existing instructions as applicable to the respective bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: A bank that is not required by the listing requirements or otherwise to publish quarterly / half-
yearly statements, shall make the disclosures for the full year in the annual financial statements.
requirements.object: the full year in the annual financial statements

conditions: If a bank has no exposure to country risk in both the current and previous year
requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Exposures
Note: If a bank has no exposure to country risk in both the current and previous year, it may omit
disclosure of the table while mentioning that it has no exposure to country risk.
requirements.object: table while mentioning that it has no exposure to country risk

requirements.full_sentence:
Aggregate value of outstanding contracts and arrangements involving related parties as on 31st March
]
1Inserted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Financial Statements:
Presentation and Disclosures) - Amendment Directions, 2026 dated January 5, 2026
<image>
(6) Concentration of deposits, advances, exposures and NPAs
Note: A bank that has not entered into any derivative transactions, both in the current and previous year
may omit these disclosures and instead disclose that it has not entered into any transactions in
derivatives in the current and previous years.
requirements.object: instead disclose that it has not entered into any transactions in derivatives in the current and previous years

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: Nature and terms of the swaps including information on credit and market risk and the
accounting policies adopted for recording the swaps shall also be disclosed.

(ii) Concentration of advances*
(Amount in ₹ crore) Particulars Current Year Previous Year Total advances to the twenty largest borrowers
Percentage of advances to twenty largest borrowers to total advances of the bank
*Advances shall be computed based on credit exposure i.e., funded and non-funded limits including
derivative exposures where applicable.
requirements.object: credit exposure i

requirements.full_sentence:
The sanctioned limits or outstanding, whichever are higher,
shall be reckoned.

conditions:
where there is no scope for redrawal
of any portion of the sanctioned limit
requirements.full_sentence:
However, in the case of fully drawn term loans, where there is no scope for redrawal
of any portion of the sanctioned limit, a bank may reckon the outstanding as the credit exposure.
requirements.object: the credit exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Concentration of exposures**
(Amount in ₹ crore) Particulars Current Year Previous Year
Total exposure to the twenty largest borrowers / customers
Percentage of exposures to the twenty largest borrowers / customers to the total exposure of the bank on borrowers / customers
**Exposures shall be computed as per applicable RBI regulation.
requirements.object: applicable RBI regulation

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Penalties imposed by the RBI under the provisions of the (a) BR Act, 1949, (b)
Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007 and (iii) Government Securities Act,
2006 (for bouncing of SGL) shall be disclosed in the 'Notes to Accounts' to the
balance sheet in the concerned bank's next Annual Report.
requirements.object: 'Notes to Accounts' to the balance sheet in the concerned bank's next Annual Report

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) A bank shall make appropriate disclosures on the nature of the breach, number of
instances of default and the quantum of penalty imposed.
requirements.object: the nature of the breach, number of instances of default and the quantum of penalty imposed

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) The defaulting participant in a reverse repo transaction shall make appropriate
disclosure on the number of instances of default as well as the quantum of penalty
paid to the RBI during the financial year.
requirements.object: the number of instances of default as well as the quantum of penalty paid to the RBI during the financial year

requirements.full_sentence:
4 For the purpose of computation of business per employee (deposits plus advances), inter-bank
deposits shall be excluded.

(ii) Bancassurance business: The details of fees / brokerage earned in
respect of insurance broking, agency and bancassurance business undertaken
by a bank shall be disclosed for both the current year and previous year.
requirements.object: the current year and previous year

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Marketing and distribution: A bank shall disclose the details of fees /
remuneration received in respect of the marketing and distribution function
(excluding bancassurance business) undertaken by it.
requirements.object: fees / remuneration received in respect of the marketing and distribution function (excluding bancassurance business) undertak

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Disclosures regarding Priority Sector Lending Certificates (PSLCs):
The amount of PSLCs (category-wise) sold and purchased during the year shall
be disclosed.

(vi) Payment of DICGC Insurance Premium
2[A bank shall disclose in the annual report that 'deposit insurance premium
as applicable was paid to DICGC within the prescribed timelines'.
requirements.object: report that 'deposit insurance premium as applicable was paid to DICGC within the prescribed timelines'

requirements.full_sentence:
In case
the bank has not paid as per the required timelines, the same shall also be
disclosed.]

(vii) Disclosure on amortisation of additional pension liability on account
of implementation of Pension Scheme in RRBs with effect from November
1, 1993
An RRB which is required to implement the RRB (Employee) Pension Scheme
with effect from November 1, 1993 may take the following course of action in the
matter:
requirements.object: (Employee) Pension Scheme with effect from November 1, 1993 may take the following course of action in the matter:

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) The liability on account of applicability of pension scheme shall be fully
recognised as per the applicable accounting standards.
requirements.object: per the applicable accounting standards

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) Appropriate disclosure of the accounting policy followed in this regard shall be
made in the 'Notes to Accounts' to the financial statements.
requirements.object: 'Notes to Accounts' to the financial statements

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall
also disclose the amount of unamortised expenditure and the consequential
net profit if the unamortised expenditure had been fully recognised in the Profit
& Loss Account.
requirements.object: of unamortised expenditure and the consequential net profit if the unamortised expenditure had been fully recognised in the P

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall adhere to following guidelines for unreconciled inter-branch account
entries.
requirements.object: for unreconciled inter-branch account entries

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) The bank shall segregate the credit entries outstanding for more than five years
in the inter-branch account and transfer them to a separate 'Blocked Account'
which shall be shown under 'Other Liabilities and Provisions - Others'.
requirements.object: outstanding for more than five years in the inter-branch account and transfer them to a separate 'Blocked Account' which

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Any adjustment from the Blocked Account should be permitted only with the
authorisation of two officials, one of whom should be from the Controlling / Head
Office if the amount exceeds ₹ One lakh.
requirements.object: the authorisation of two officials, one of whom should be from the Controlling / Head Office if the amount exceeds ₹ One lakh

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The balance in Blocked Account shall be reckoned as a liability for the purpose of
the maintenance of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) and Statutory Liquidity Ratio
(SLR).
requirements.object: liability for the purpose of the maintenance of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) and Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR)

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The bank shall maintain category-wise (head-wise) accounts for various types of
transactions put through inter-branch accounts, so that the netting can be done
category-wise.
requirements.object: for various types of transactions put through inter-branch accounts, so that the netting can be done categ

requirements.full_sentence:
As on the balance sheet date, the bank shall segregate the debit
and credit entries remaining unreconciled for more than six months and arrive at
the net position category-wise, while also considering the balance in the Blocked
Account.
requirements.object: credit entries remaining unreconciled for more than six months and arrive at the net position category-wise, while also consid

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The net debit under all the categories of inter-branch accounts shall be
aggregated and a provision equivalent to 100 per cent of the aggregate net debit
shall be made.
requirements.object: provision equivalent to 100 per cent of the aggregate net debit shall be made

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the bank shall ensure that the net debit in one category is not set-
off against net credit in another category.
requirements.object: debit in one category is not set- off against net credit in another category

requirements.full_sentence:
Treatment of outstanding entries in Nostro accounts shall of a bank shall be as
under.
requirements.object: be as under

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(1) The bank shall take steps to have a strong control over reconciliation and put in
place a system of real-time reconciliation, which provides for immediate
escalation of differences, if any.
requirements.object: a strong control over reconciliation and put in place a system of real-time reconciliation, which provides for immediate escalation of differences

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) There shall be close monitoring of pending items in Nostro accounts by top
management at short intervals.
requirements.object: pending items in Nostro accounts by top management at short intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) All unreconciled credit entries in Nostro accounts which are outstanding for more
than three years shall be transferred to a Blocked Account and shown as
outstanding liabilities.
requirements.object: Blocked Account and shown as outstanding liabilities

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The balance in the Blocked Account shall be reckoned for the purpose of CRR /
SLR.
requirements.object: purpose of CRR / SLR

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) A bank shall make 100 per cent provision in respect of all unreconciled debit
entries in the Nostro accounts, which are outstanding for more than two years.
requirements.object: provision in respect of all unreconciled debit entries in the Nostro accounts, which are outstanding for more than two years

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Section 17(1) of the BR Act, 1949 a bank is required to transfer, out of
the balance of profit as disclosed in the profit and loss account, a sum equivalent
to not less than 20 per cent of such profit to Reserve Fund.
requirements.object: the balance of profit as disclosed in the profit and loss account, a sum equivalent to not less than 20 per cent of such profit to

requirements.full_sentence:
Unless specifically allowed by extant regulations, the bank shall take prior
approval from the RBI before any appropriation is made from the Statutory
Reserve or any other reserve.
requirements.object: the RBI before any appropriation is made from the Statutory Reserve or any other reserve

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks are further advised that,
(1) all expenses including provisions and write-offs recognized in a period, whether
mandatory or prudential, shall be reflected in the profit and loss account for the
period as an 'above the line' item (i.e., before arriving at the net profit / loss for
the year);
requirements.object: profit and loss account for the period as an 'above the line' item (i

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) draw down from reserves, with the prior approval of RBI, shall be effected only
'below the line' (i.e.
requirements.object: the line' (i

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(3) suitable disclosures shall be made of such draw down in the 'Notes toAccounts'
to the Balance Sheet.
requirements.object: draw down in the 'Notes toAccounts' to the Balance Sheet

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, the share premium account shall not be utilised for writing off the
expenses relating to the issue of debt instruments.
requirements.object: off the expenses relating to the issue of debt instruments

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: For the purposes of this Direction, issue expenses shall include
registration and other regulatory fees, payments made to legal, accounting, and
other professional advisers, printing costs, and stamp duties.
requirements.object: regulatory fees, payments made to legal, accounting, and other professional advisers, printing costs, and stamp duties

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of provisioning for frauds, a bank that has reported the fraud within the
prescribed time shall have the option to make the provision for the same over a
period, not exceeding four quarters, commencing from the quarter in which the
fraud has been detected.
requirements.object: make the provision for the same over a period, not exceeding four quarters, commencing from the quarter in which the fraud has been

requirements.full_sentence:
Where the bank chooses to provide for the fraud over two to four quarters and
this results in the full provisioning being made in more than one financial year,
subject to compliance with applicable laws, it may debit reserves other than the
Statutory Reserve by the amount remaining un-provided at the end of the
financial year by credit to provisions.
requirements.object: the Statutory Reserve by the amount remaining un-provided at the end of the financial year by credit to provisions

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that it should subsequently reverse the debits to the reserves
proportionately and complete the provisioning by debiting profit and loss account,
in the successive quarters of the next financial year.
requirements.object: the debits to the reserves proportionately and complete the provisioning by debiting profit and loss account, in the su

requirements.full_sentence:
Where there has been delay, beyond the prescribed period, in reporting the fraud
to the RBI, the entire provisioning is required to be made at once.
requirements.object: once

requirements.full_sentence:
Unreconciled credit balances in any transitory account representing unclaimed
balances shall not be transferred to the profit and loss account or to any
reserves.
requirements.object: profit and loss account or to any reserves

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall make provisions for DTL on the Special Reserve created under
Section 36(1)(viii) of Income Tax Act, 1961.
requirements.object: on the Special Reserve created under Section 36(1)(viii) of Income Tax Act, 1961

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that balance sheet and profit and loss account reflects true
and fair picture of its financial position.
requirements.object: and profit and loss account reflects true and fair picture of its financial position

requirements.full_sentence:
Instances of window dressing of financials, short provisioning, misclassification of
NPAs, under-reporting / incorrect computation of exposure / risk weight, incorrect
capitalisation of expenses, capitalisation of interest on NPAs, deliberate inflation
of asset and liabilities at the end of the financial year and subsequent reversal
immediately in next financial year, etc., shall be viewed seriously and appropriate
penal action in terms of the provisions of the BR Act, 1949 shall be considered.
requirements.object: appropriate penal action in terms of the provisions of the BR Act, 1949 shall be considered

requirements.full_sentence:
The
Directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been
taken, or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines
shall continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the repeal of these directions,
instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially affect:
a.
requirements.object: affect: a

requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy
may be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty,
forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions,
instructions, or guidelines had not been repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
For Banks incorporated outside India Capital (i) The amount brought in by banks by way of start-up capital as prescribed by RBI should be shown under this head.
requirements.object: head

requirements.full_sentence:
Others *
Total
* In case there is any unadjusted balance of loss the same may be shown under this item with appropriate foot-.
requirements.object: item with appropriate foot-

requirements.full_sentence:
Miscellaneous Income
Total
Note: Under items II to V loss figures shall be shown in brackets
